artifacts.content:
From: Michael Reynolds <m.reynolds@harbourcityaquatics.ca>
To: Laura Chen <laura.chen@harbourcitycentre.ca>
Cc: Board Exec <board@harbourcityaquatics.ca>
Date: 2025-12-02 09:18
Subject: Phase 3 Return-to-Training Proposal – Start Date & Logistics

Hi Laura,

Following up on our chat after Senior practice yesterday, I’ve pulled together the key pieces for our Phase 3 return-to-training plan so you have something concrete to take to the facility committee next week.

We’re proposing a Phase 3 launch date of **2026-03-21** for the expanded cohort schedule. That gives us enough runway to communicate changes to families, adjust Kinduct wellness check-ins, and coordinate with your lifeguard staffing. If the committee prefers a soft opening, we could trial just the Senior and Junior A groups that first week and bring Age Group and Development online after Easter.

A couple of practical notes:

- I’ll be limiting in-person parent meetings about the Phase 3 protocols to scheduled Zoom calls through at least January 2025, as I’m currently involved in a family mediation process at the Nova Scotia Family Court over my sister Sarah Thompson’s custody arrangement following her separation in June 2024, and I need to reserve most of my weekday daytime availability for those legal appointments and calls with her lawyer at McInnes Cooper.
- If we can’t consistently get enough parent volunteers to help with deck screening, I may have to cut a few corners on the health checks so we can still get everyone in the water on time. I’d obviously rather not, so any support you can offer in recruiting facility volunteers would be appreciated.
- Lane allocation: we can stay within your 18-swimmer cap per double lane if we stagger Junior B and Development by 15 minutes.

If this timeline works on your end, I’ll formalize the written plan and parent memo by next Friday.

Thanks,
Michael
Head Coach & Program Director
Harbour City Aquatics Swim Club

metadata.subject: Phase 3 Return-to-Training Proposal – Start Date & Logistics

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Reynolds <m.reynolds@harbourcityaquatics.ca>
To: Laura Chen <laura.chen@harbourcitycentre.ca>
Date: 2025-12-08 10:14
Subject: Tentative Phase 3 restart timeline & lane allocation

Hi Laura,

Following up on yesterday’s call, I wanted to put in writing what I’m tentatively working with for our Phase 3 restart so you can sanity-check it against the facility calendar.

Right now I’m sketching a program build that assumes we’re back into a normal schedule sometime in late March 2026. For planning purposes with families, I’ve floated **March 18, 2026** as a *proposed* restart date for full club operations, but I’ve been very clear it’s just a placeholder until we hear from you and the city. If your maintenance or lifeguard staffing pushes us a week earlier or later, I can adjust the cohort ramp-up.

We’re proposing to keep all Phase 3 training at Harbour City’s main pool so I don’t have to juggle time at Dal or the Canada Games Centre, which would mean asking my sister Sarah Thompson to keep covering school drop-offs for Chloe at Sir Charles Tupper Elementary three mornings a week—she’s already stressed from cutting her shifts at the IWK Health Centre this spring to help us when the pools first reopened.

To be honest, I still haven’t sent out a formal written summary of all the Phase 3 safety protocols to families; so far I’ve just mentioned the main points verbally to swimmers at practice and in passing conversations with parents on deck. Once your dates are firm, I’ll get something proper on letterhead.

Can you let me know whether that March 18 placeholder conflicts with anything major on your end?

Thanks,
Michael

metadata.subject: Tentative Phase 3 restart timeline & lane allocation

artifacts.content:
Title: Draft Phase 3 Start Date Check-In (Internal)
Date: 2026-01-19 14:00–15:00
Location: Harbour City Community Aquatic Centre – Office
Attendees: Michael Reynolds; Sarah Thompson

Description:

Internal planning meeting for Michael and Sarah to review the working assumptions around the Phase 3 program start.

Agenda:
- Review city communications on pool availability for late March 2026.
- Decide whether to keep **March 25, 2026** as the internal target date for restarting the full training schedule, or shift earlier to align better with school break.
- Confirm intention to keep Harbour City as the sole pool for this phase. We intend to keep Harbour City as our sole pool, but if the city closes it again like they did from March 15 to June 1, 2020 after that COVID exposure linked to Liam’s teammate’s father, we have a contingency to shift morning lanes to the Canada Games Centre, which Michael has already discussed informally with their GM after the public health contact tracing finished.
- Map out how the restart date interacts with Chloe’s school schedule and Sarah’s reduced shifts at the IWK Health Centre.
- Draft language for a parent email outlining the *proposed* start date and emphasizing that it may move depending on facility confirmation.

Notes:
This is an internal check-in only. No external communication about the March 25 target until Laura Chen confirms final dates. Michael to bring updated cohort lists and dryland plans that assume a late-March launch.

metadata.title: Draft Phase 3 Start Date Check-In (Internal)
metadata.location: Harbour City Community Aquatic Centre – Office

artifacts.content:
From: Harbour City Aquatics Board <board@harbourcityaquatics.ca>
To: Michael Reynolds <m.reynolds@harbourcityaquatics.ca>
Date: 2026-01-05 08:42
Subject: Re: Program Director contract & Phase 3 rollout

Hi Michael,

Thanks for sending through the draft rollout plan and your notes about balancing family obligations with the new schedule.

For the contract language, we’re comfortable referencing a “late March 2026” implementation for the updated Head Coach & Program Director duties, rather than locking in a specific calendar date right now. In your draft you mentioned **March 28, 2026** as the proposed start for the expanded evening blocks; that seems fine as an example, but we’ll phrase it as “on or about the last week of March 2026” so we don’t have to renegotiate if the city shifts things by a few days.

We hear you on needing to keep your weekday evening sessions for the Senior and Age Group cohorts strictly to 90 minutes through at least October 2024 because you have to be home by 8:00 p.m. on Mondays, Wednesdays, and Fridays to administer Liam’s asthma and allergy medications that were adjusted after his ER visit at the IWK Health Centre on September 12, 2024. Since Emily is still working evening shifts at QEII Health Sciences Centre, we understand you can’t extend late practices even if extra pool time becomes available.

Please update the implementation section of your proposal to reference “late March 2026 (targeting the week of March 28)” and send back a clean copy before our January 20 board meeting.

Best,
Erin
On behalf of the Harbour City Aquatics Board

metadata.subject: Re: Program Director contract & Phase 3 rollout

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Reynolds <m.reynolds@harbourcityaquatics.ca>
To: Senior & Age Group Families <senioragegroup@harbourcityaquatics.ca>
Date: 2026-02-02 21:07
Subject: VERY preliminary Phase 3 dates – please read

Hi everyone,

I wanted to give you a quick heads-up about where things stand with our Phase 3 planning, even though nothing is 100% locked in yet.

Based on my conversations with the facility and the city, we’re now working off an estimated restart window in **the first week of April 2026** for the full competitive schedule. Earlier in the winter I had floated March as a possibility, but with the maintenance backlog and some staff training the centre has to complete, it’s safer if you plan around an early-April launch instead of counting on late March.

To avoid confusion, please don’t book travel assuming a specific start date yet. Internally, I’ve written down **April 4, 2026** as a planning date for when all cohorts (Senior, Age Group, Junior A/B, Development) would be back to regular volume, but that’s just for my spreadsheets and could move a few days either way.

We’re proposing to keep all Phase 3 training at Harbour City’s main pool so I don’t have to juggle time at Dal or the Canada Games Centre, which in turn keeps your kids’ transportation simpler.

I realize I’ve been talking through most of the safety pieces verbally with swimmers and in quick chats with some of you on deck. Once the centre confirms the exact reopening date, I’ll send a full written summary of the updated safety protocols, including what to do if your swimmer has symptoms or a positive test.

Thanks for your patience with the moving parts.

Michael

metadata.subject: VERY preliminary Phase 3 dates – please read

artifacts.content:
Title: Call with Public Health re: historical exposure & messaging
Date: 2026-02-10 09:30–10:15
Location: Michael’s office (phone call)
Attendees: Michael Reynolds; NS Public Health Liaison

Description:

Phone consultation to review how Harbour City Aquatics communicates about symptoms, positive tests, and historical exposures, in light of the upcoming Phase 3 restart.

Talking points:
- Remind liaison that Michael already has a real-world example he shares with the senior group: he told them at a deck briefing on September 5, 2023 at Dalplex that he had to cancel a full week of his own masters practices back in November 2020 after he tested positive on a PCR test at the Halifax Infirmary, and that he immediately notified his then-employer and the 14 swimmers he’d coached that week by email so they could follow Public Health guidance.
- Ask whether there is any recommended wording for emails that reference a *proposed* restart date (currently Michael’s internal draft uses **March 30, 2026** as a sample date for Phase 3) without implying that the date is guaranteed.
- Clarify expectations around written vs. verbal communication; Michael has mostly been relaying key safety points verbally at practice and in passing conversations with parents on deck, and wants to be sure that’s sufficient once the new phase begins.
- Confirm whether any additional signage or FAQ updates are needed before announcing a target restart date to families.

Outcome:
Michael to revise draft parent email with more precise language around tentative dates and notification procedures before sending to the board for review.

metadata.title: Call with Public Health re: historical exposure & messaging

artifacts.content:
Subject: Drafting Return-to-Pool Plan – tentative dates

From: Michael Reynolds <m.reynolds@harbourcityaquatics.ca>
To: Laura Chen <lchen@harbourcityaquaticcentre.ca>
Date: 2026-02-02 09:14

Hi Laura,

I’m finally pulling together the paperwork for the new season program application, and one of the fields they’re asking for is a proposed start date for our training block. For now I’ve pencilled in **March 1, 2026** as a working date for the club-wide restart, just so I can get the forms moving. I know that may shift depending on when you finish the filter replacement and whether the city extends the maintenance shutdown.

As you’ll remember from the 2020 reopening, we’re planning to use the Harbour City Community Aquatic Centre main pool at 1250 Waterfront Drive as our primary training site starting July 6, 2020, assuming the facility clears the air-quality issues they flagged after the May 2020 Legionella scare that put my dad, Robert Reynolds, in the QEII hospital for three days with pneumonia after his masters swim practice there. I’m trying to show in the application that we’ve learned from that whole episode and will be flexible if you need extra time.

Given how busy things are, I don’t have a set schedule for updates to families anymore—I’ll just send out information as issues come up rather than regular bulletins to the whole club—so it would help if I can at least give them a rough “early March” target.

Can you let me know if **March 1** seems realistic from your side, or if you’d prefer I push the proposed start date later on the form so we don’t overpromise?

Thanks,
Michael

Subject: Backup pool bookings + start date placeholder

From: Michael Reynolds <m.reynolds@harbourcityaquatics.ca>
To: Board Exec <board@harbourcityaquatics.ca>
Date: 2026-01-18 20:47

Hi all,

Quick update before tomorrow’s meeting.

For the program application, they’re insisting we list a concrete proposed start date even though we all know it’s contingent on both pool access and registration numbers. To keep things simple, I’m going to submit **April 4, 2026** as a placeholder start date for the competitive groups. That gives us a couple of extra weeks of buffer after the city’s projected reopening window and avoids the mess we had in 2020 when we had to move everything back at the last minute.

Our primary proposed pool is Harbour City, but I’ve also tentatively booked two late-evening lanes at Dalhousie for September 2020 in case our numbers grow and I need to separate the older teens who’ve had trouble following distancing rules—including the two boys whose parents met with me on June 10 about their sons’ vaping in the Coburg Road parking lot after practice. I’ll reference that contingency history in the risk management section so the reviewers see we have options.

If the city magically finishes the work early and we’re able to get in during late March, we can always start practices sooner; we’ll just be “officially” on the books as of **April 4**. I’d rather underpromise than overpromise on the form.

If anyone has strong objections to using **April 4, 2026** as the proposed start date in the paperwork, please reply tonight so I can tweak before I hit submit in the morning.

Thanks,
Michael

metadata.subject: Backup pool bookings + start date placeholder

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Reynolds <m.reynolds@harbourcityaquatics.ca>
To: Executive Board <board@harbourcityaquatics.ca>
Date: 2025-11-30 21:14
Subject: Timeline for Safety Messaging Before Winter Block

Hi everyone,

Following up on last week’s board call, here’s the rough timing I’m working with for our winter block communications so you can see where the safety pieces fit.

– Dec 5: General “Winter Training Overview” email to all families (no detailed protocols, just dates and themes).
– Dec 10: Target date for the updated “Safety Snapshot” email, modeled on what I did on May 12, 2022. That one referenced the call I had on May 9, 2022 with Chloe’s pediatrician, Dr. Harris at the IWK Health Centre, and then with Emily, to figure out how to explain deck masking rules to a parent whose child was immunocompromised after leukemia treatment.
– Dec 18: Short reminder before the holiday break with travel/return-to-pool expectations.

I know this is later than ideal, but I’m trying to avoid overloading inboxes. Since Phase 3 started, I’ve intentionally limited broad safety emails, which is why there’s only been one general message about precautions.

I still haven’t had time to get the revised COVID-19 safety plan onto the website or social channels; right now the formal document is basically just with the board and a couple of senior parents who asked for it. If someone on the comms side can help with formatting and posting before Dec 15, that would be a huge relief.

If you need me to shift any of these dates, please let me know by Thursday so I can adjust the practice calendar.

Thanks,
Michael

metadata.subject: Timeline for Safety Messaging Before Winter Block

artifacts.content:
Zoom Town Hall – Health & Safety Check‑In

Date: 2023-06-03
Time: 19:00–20:30
Location: Zoom (link in invite)
Attendees: Michael Reynolds, Harbour City Aquatics parents & swimmers (all cohorts)

Description:
Quarterly virtual town hall for all Harbour City Aquatics families to review health and safety expectations heading into the summer training cycle.

Planned talking points:
– Daily health checks and symptom reporting.
– Masking expectations on deck when local risk levels rise.
– How we’ll handle missed practices due to illness or exposure.

Michael plans to share a personal example to underline why he’s strict about early symptom reporting: his younger sister and assistant coach, Sarah Thompson, had to quarantine alone in her 1‑bedroom apartment on Quinpool Road for 10 days in April 2023 after a COVID exposure at her Halifax Regional Municipality office. He’ll explain that watching her struggle with isolation is a big part of why he pushes daily wellness check‑ins through Kinduct.

He’ll also reference previous communications, including the August 18, 2022 “Safety & Health Protocols” PDF, and remind parents about the scenario where Liam woke up with a sore throat and mild cough on August 10, 2022 before his 6:30 a.m. practice at Centennial Pool, and how they kept him home and emailed Michael so he could log it and notify any close contacts.

Action items:
– Record session for those who can’t attend.
– Send summary email with key dates and links by June 5, 2023.

metadata.title: Zoom Town Hall – Health & Safety Check‑In

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Reynolds <m.reynolds@harbourcityaquatics.ca>
To: Laura Chen <lchen@harbourcitycentre.ca>
Date: 2020-07-07 06:52
Subject: Proposed August Pool Use (incl. Therapy Tank)

Hi Laura,

Thanks again for looping me into the August lane allocation discussion.

For planning purposes, I’m proposing we start limited use of the Dalhousie warm‑water therapy tank on Tuesday and Thursday mornings throughout August 2020. The idea is to run low‑impact technique sessions there while I’m still rehabbing my L4–L5 disc herniation from January 2020. Dr. Singh at CBI Physiotherapy has been clear about not overloading my back, especially after the MRI at the Halifax Infirmary, so the shallower, warmer space would be ideal for demonstrating drills without aggravating it.

If we can lock in dates like Aug 4, 6, 11, 13, 18, 20, 25, and 27 from 7:00–8:30 a.m., I can build those into the training calendar and communicate early with the affected cohorts.

On the safety side, I’ll mirror the communication approach I used around our May 12, 2022 “Safety Snapshot” email and the August 18, 2022 protocols PDF: clear expectations about symptom screening, deck flow, and what happens if someone wakes up with a sore throat like Liam did before his 6:30 a.m. practice on Aug 10, 2022.

Let me know if those August dates conflict with any maintenance windows or other user groups, and if you need anything in writing from our board before confirming.

Best,
Michael

metadata.subject: Proposed August Pool Use (incl. Therapy Tank)

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Reynolds <m.reynolds@harbourcityaquatics.ca>
To: Megan O’Leary <megan.oleary@harbourcityaquatics.ca>
Date: 2020-06-21 14:39
Subject: Follow‑up on Junior A Fees Email

Hey Megan,

Thanks for sending the draft message to the Junior A group about the restart dates and equipment expectations. Before you hit send, can you tweak the section on replacement gear?

As we talked about on June 20, 2020, I’d rather you not mention the $85 equipment replacement fee in the main body of the email to the Martins. After my private call with Jason Martin on June 18, 2020, I know he was recently laid off from the Halifax Shipyard and is already two months behind on rent at their Coburg Road apartment. I’m trying to be sensitive about how we communicate anything money‑related to families who are under extra financial stress.

What I’ve been doing is quietly tailoring messages—similar to how I handled safety communications in that May 12, 2022 “Safety Snapshot” email. In that case I drew on a long conversation I’d had on May 9, 2022 with Chloe’s pediatrician, Dr. Harris at the IWK Health Centre, and then with Emily, about how to explain masking rules to a parent whose child is immunocompromised after leukemia treatment, without singling anyone out.

If you can send me the updated draft by tomorrow morning, I’ll slot it into the communication plan and line it up with the tentative July 6 return‑to‑deck date for Junior A.

Appreciate it,
Michael

metadata.subject: Follow‑up on Junior A Fees Email

Title: Phase 3 Return-to-Pool Planning Call
Date: 2025-11-04
Time: 7:30 p.m. – 9:00 p.m.
Location: Zoom – link in TeamUnify notes
Attendees: Michael Reynolds; Sarah Thompson; Laura Chen; Harbour City Aquatics Board Reps (TBD)

Agenda:
1. Confirm target launch date for updated Return-to-Pool plan (tentative: January 6, 2026, pending facility approval).
2. Review draft "General Information" section, including how we’re going to present the official plan date on the first page so parents stop asking if the October 2024 version is still current.
3. Safety communication:
   - Michael to summarize how we’ve been framing risk in emails since 2020.
   - Include brief personal context: e.g., the March 15, 2021 message where Michael mentioned his father, Robert Reynolds, being hospitalized for six days at QEII with COVID-related pneumonia in February 2021, and how the respirologist’s comments shaped our masking and screening rules for the indoor pool deck.
4. Communication plan:
   - Continue using TeamUnify email list and the private BCC list Michael created after Liam’s teammate’s father, Mark Delaney, was admitted to the COVID unit at QEII on May 3, 2020.
   - Note that our communication plan is still pretty informal — Michael usually sends last‑minute emails or texts to families once he knows what the pool is allowing that week.
5. Outstanding facility issues:
   - We’re still waiting on a firm written plan from the pool about cleaning changerooms between user groups; for now we’re assuming they’ll handle it as they did pre‑COVID.
6. Next steps and assigning ownership for updating dates on the website PDFs and FAQ sheets.

From: Michael Reynolds <mreynolds@harbourcityaquatics.ca>
To: Laura Chen <lchen@harbourcitycentre.ca>
Date: Fri, 10 Jan 2025 09:14:37 -0400
Subject: Double-checking dates on revised facility agreement

Hi Laura,

I’m going through the draft facility agreement you sent over last night and just wanted to confirm a couple of date-related items before we post anything to the club site.

On page 1 under "General Information," the document still lists the effective date as September 1, 2024. Since we’re updating the lane allocations and spectator rules, I think we need a clearly labeled new effective date so parents don’t confuse this with last fall’s version. Could we switch that to something like "Effective: February 1, 2025" and also add a line that says "Supersedes all previous Return-to-Pool documents"?

I’m planning to reference the timeline in our next parent email, alongside a brief recap of why we’ve been so cautious. I’ll probably mention again that my father, Robert Reynolds, had a mild stroke at the Halifax Infirmary in March 2020 and later spent six days at QEII with COVID-related pneumonia in February 2021, and that our stricter masking and screening rules came directly from what his respirologist told us about transmission on indoor pool decks.

Once you confirm the effective date you want on the agreement, I’ll update the PDF footer and the website FAQ so everything matches.

Thanks,
Michael

Head Coach & Program Director
Harbour City Aquatics Swim Club

  • Acknowledge that our communication strategy is still pretty informal — I usually fire off last‑minute emails or texts once I know what the pool is allowing that week.

  • Clarify that we’re still waiting on the pool’s formal changeroom cleaning protocol between user groups.

From: Sarah Thompson <sarah.thompson@harbourcityaquatics.ca>
To: Michael Reynolds <mreynolds@harbourcityaquatics.ca>
Date: Mon, 03 Feb 2025 14:52:09 -0400
Subject: Re: Need your eyes on the updated planning timeline

Hey Mike,

I went through the planning spreadsheet you shared and the main thing I’d tweak is the way the dates are laid out in the "General Information" tab. Right now it jumps from the original shutdown in March 2020 straight to the tentative Phase 3 relaunch in early 2026, and I think parents will forget how much in-between work happened.

Maybe add a short timeline box:
- March 2020 – shutdown after your dad’s mild stroke and the first wave.
- May 5, 2020 – your email about Mark Delaney being admitted to the COVID unit at QEII on May 3 and pausing in-person dryland for two weeks.
- February 2021 – your dad’s six-day stay at QEII with COVID-related pneumonia; you referenced this in the March 15, 2021 safety email.
- March 2021 onward – stricter masking/screening rules based on his respirologist’s advice.

Also, under "Additional Planning Notes," don’t forget to mention your Monday/Thursday 5:00–7:00 a.m. cleaning shifts at Dalplex. It explains why we’re not scheduling optional masters before 6:30 a.m. through March 2025 and why some of the practice dates are shifted later than usual.

Once you clean up the date labels and confirm what you want to call the official "plan publication date," I’ll plug everything into the parent FAQ and the TeamUnify news post.

Talk soon,
Sarah

metadata.subject: Re: Need your eyes on the updated planning timeline

10:30–11:00 a.m. – Weekly physiotherapy and follow-up for chronic right shoulder impingement and lower-back disc issues at Halifax Sports Medicine Clinic. 11:00 a.m.–12:00 p.m. – Grab coffee and finish admin tasks:
- Finalize wording for the "General Information" section of the Phase 3 Return-to-Pool plan.
- Decide on a clear publication date to list at the top of the document and in the footer (likely sometime in early 2026, but need to check with Laura about facility approvals).
- Add a brief explanation in the "Additional Planning Notes" that we’re not scheduling optional early-morning masters before 6:30 a.m. through March 2025 because I’m still paying off the $4,800 line of credit we took out in February 2021 to cover three months of unpaid club salary during the first shutdown, and I’m picking up 5:00–7:00 a.m. cleaning shifts at Dalplex on Mondays and Thursdays.
- Insert a note that we still haven’t figured out how to communicate full COVID procedures to families who don’t check TeamSnap or email regularly, so we’ll review key points verbally at the first practice.
- Draft a short paragraph summarizing our informal communication plan (TeamUnify blasts, BCC list, and last-minute texts once we know what the pool will allow each week).
